prose:
Method–provenance block (reproducibility details)
Property: Fukui reactivity indices of furan (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and identification of the most electrophilic site (headline value = f⁺ on atom 5)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF v?.
Geometry provenance: single-point charges evaluated on the fixed Cartesian coordinates supplied in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz; no re-optimization was performed.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (neutral singlet). N±1 states for f⁺/f⁻ computed as cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge –1, multiplicity 2).
Software backend: PySCF (through chemkit fukui-reactivity skill); default RI/JK density fitting active.
Convergence & warnings: The run completed with integrity.status = "ok" and integrity.trustworthy = true; the engine’s `warnings` array was empty (no warnings reported). Integrity checks flagged as "warning" severity all passed (sum of f⁺ = 1.0000; sum of f⁻ = 1.0000; schema header present).
Live log: computation executed via chemkit CLI (see cli_invocation in JSON); live output was written during the run to the autogenerated .out file in the caller’s working directory.

Result
• Most electrophilic atom: C5 (index 4) with f⁺ = 1.9242931 (dimensionless Mulliken Fukui index).
• Most nucleophilic atom: C5 (index 4) with f⁻ = 0.1616371.

The headline number above (1.9242931) is the largest f⁺ value returned directly by the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill at B3LYP/def2-TZVP, reported here without rounding.
